items.title: Total repression and air strikes bring unrelenting dread for Iranians
items.summary: Tehran residents tell the BBC they're caught between US-Israeli bombing and an Iranian regime trying to reassert its power.

items.title: Death of Ali Larijani deepens crisis at heart of Iran's leadership
items.summary: Larijani has long been seen as one of the Islamic Republic's most experienced and influential policy makers.

items.title: Top US counterterrorism official resigns over Iran war, urging Trump to 'reverse course'
items.summary: National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent says Trump "started this war due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby".

items.title: India's cheap weight-loss drugs could reshape global obesity fight
items.summary: With India’s semaglutide patent expiring on 20 March, about 50 branded generics are expected to enter the market.

items.title: US judge orders Trump administration to reopen Voice of America
items.summary: Some 85% of VOA staff were laid off by Trump who accused the international broadcaster of bias.

items.title: Air strike hit Kabul rehab centre as patients ate dinner, survivor tells BBC
items.summary: The exact number killed in Monday night's strike by Pakistan is still not known, but is feared to be in the hundreds.

items.title: Italy warns Russian tanker could explode in Mediterranean at any time
items.summary: Italy is one of nine EU countries urging the European Commission to act over the Arctic Metagaz, drifting out of control in the Mediterranean Sea.

items.title: Spanish king reopens debate on conquest of Mexico by acknowledging 'abuse'
items.summary: King Felipe appears to have helped thaw frosty relations with Mexico while reviving a fierce debate over events 500 years ago.

items.title: US Attorney General Bondi formally summoned to Congress in Epstein case
items.summary: It comes weeks after a Republican lawmaker accused the justice department of a "cover-up" in releasing the Epstein files.

items.title: Water supply cut in Moldova after oil spill blamed on Russian strike in Ukraine
items.summary: Moldova has imposed a 15-day state of alert in the Dniester river basin as a precaution.

items.title: Pakistan air strike kills at least 100 at Kabul drug rehab centre
items.summary: Some of the bodies were injured beyond recognition, forensic laboratory sources told the BBC.

items.title: Chile's president begins building border barrier less than week into term
items.summary: José Antonio Kast visited the ditch - a few feet wide and deep - on Monday, calling it a "milestone".

items.title: Oh, you think the government will regulate Kalshi and Polymarket? Wanna bet?
items.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has a problem: It's not very good at policing insider trading. And insider trading has become a pressing concern for prediction markets. Even Kalshi's recently publicized fines for insider trading - levied against a politician and an employee of YouTube influencer MrBeast - were effectively self-policing. The exchange says it's […]

items.title: The hits keep coming in Spider-Man: Brand New Day’s first trailer
items.summary: The first trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day is finally here, and it looks like Marvel and Sony are about to put Peter Parker through a different kind of existential crisis. Though nobody in Brand New Day's trailer knows who Peter (Tom Holland) is or that he's the one saving people as Spider-Man, he still […]

items.title: The great EV pullback: all the obstacles, cancellations, and delays
items.summary: The auto industry bet big on electric vehicles, but now those ambitious goals are falling apart. Demand was already slowing down when Donald Trump took office and took an ax to pro-EV policy: the elimination of the federal EV tax credit, kneecapping clean energy, and bulldozing emissions rules. Tariffs have been taking their toll as […]

items.title: Nvidia has lost the plot with gamers
items.summary: Nvidia surely thought it was doing a good thing for gamers by "upgrading" the faces of our favorite video game characters. But that just shows how much the company has lost the plot. Nvidia could've marketed its new DLSS 5 real-time lighting technology as a way to make future, next-gen games look better. Instead, it […]

items.title: DLSS 5: Has Nvidia’s AI graphics technology gone too far?
items.summary: Nvidia has revealed a new “3D guided neural rendering model” called DLSS 5 that can change a game’s lighting and materials in real-time, and… many gamers aren’t happy. From DLSS 5 memes to complaints about how it’s “yassified” Resident Evil Requiem characters in demos, the first impression has not been a good one, no matter […]

items.title: BMW brings back the i3 as a funky four-door EV
items.summary: BMW's new class of EVs begins with the iX3 SUV, but you had to know it wouldn't end there. The company's engineers didn't spend all that time working up a completely redesigned and substantially more efficient EV platform just for one crossover, and now it's time for the second wave. Meet the new i3, which […]

items.title: I went to the Pentagon to watch Pete Hegseth scold war reporters
items.summary: It is day 13 of America's surprise war with Iran - by sheer coincidence, it's Friday the 13th - and I am delirious. I haven't had a coffee since I woke up at 5AM, because I'm not allowed to bring outside beverages into the Pentagon (the security screening cutoff was at 7AM for the 8AM), […]

items.title: The Beats Studio Pro are nearly $200 off ahead of Amazon’s big spring sale
items.summary: Earlier this week, Apple unveiled the AirPods Max 2. The forthcoming over-ear headphones build on their predecessor with an H2 chip that powers a range of new features; however, they still don’t offer the sort of cross-OS compatibility you’ll get with a modern pair of Beats headphones — including the Beats Studio Pro, which are […]

items.title: Remedy’s live-service shooter Firebreak is getting its final major update
items.summary: Remedy is winding down its team shooter FBC: Firebreak with a big update that launches today. But while the game won't be getting any new content going forward, the studio intends to keep it alive for the foreseeable future. It's yet another example of a live-service game struggling amidst a volatile market. The new update […]

items.title: Spotify adds ‘Exclusive Mode’ audiophile feature for Windows PCs
items.summary: Starting Tuesday, Spotify users will have access to "Exclusive Mode," a new feature in the streaming service's Windows app that can help maximize audio quality by giving the Spotify app full control over your device's audio processing. According to Spotify, "your computer may alter audio before it reaches your DAC by resampling it, mixing other […]

items.title: Takeaways from opening week of NFL free agency: Biggest signings, losses for all 32 teams
items.summary: Let's break down the main free agency signings, latest buzz on moves and biggest remaining questions for every team.

items.title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
items.summary: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

items.title: Sources: New WNBA CBA includes $7M salary cap
items.summary: After a prolonged labor battle, the WNBA and players' union have reached a verbal agreement on the terms for a new CBA. Sources told ESPN that the salary cap will start at $7 million and raise the average salary to around $600,000.

items.title: Sources: Dolphins star Achane off-limits in trade
items.summary: The Dolphins have informed inquiring teams that De'Von Achane is not available in a trade, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Trucks driver parked for mocking IndyCar racer
items.summary: NASCAR driver Daniel Dye has been suspended -- the second known major penalty of his racing career -- for mocking IndyCar driver David Malukas during a recent livestream.

items.title: Mark saves day, sends Texas to NCAA first round
items.summary: Tramon Mark hit a fadeaway jumper from just inside the 3-point line with 1.1 seconds left, and Texas survived a late rally to beat NC State 68-66 on Tuesday night in a First Four matchup.

items.title: Other shoe drops: Caruso T'd up for desperate D
items.summary: Thunder guard Alex Caruso was called for goaltending and a technical foul for an illegal defensive move after he swatted away a layup attempt with his shoe.

items.title: Tiger 'working,' but Masters status still unclear
items.summary: Tiger Woods remained mum on his playing status for next month's Masters, telling reporters after the TGL semifinals that his health continues to be up and down.

items.title: U.S. laments another WBC loss as star offense lags
items.summary: Team USA, boasting one of the most talented rosters ever assembled, failed to live up to its billing Tuesday night and could only watch as Venezuela celebrated its first World Baseball Classic championship.

items.title: Ex-reality TV star Holmes repeats Iditarod win
items.summary: Former reality TV star Jessie Holmes cruised to a victory in the Iditarod, becoming the third competitor in the 54-year history of the race to repeat the year after winning for the first time.

items.title: 🏀 Giant Killers: Identifying men's tourney upsets
items.summary: Which double-digit seeds have the highest potential to advance to the second round? Here's what ESPN's BPI says.

items.title: Ranking the top 50 men's college basketball players who could define March Madness
items.summary: From mid-major leaders to national superstars, here's who could impact their teams' NCAA tournament runs.

sentiment.label: Neutral
sentiment.rationale: Average of 12 per-headline sentiment scores (raw avg +0.071, dampened ×0.6 → +0.0428). This is a coarse signal; interpret as overall tone, not factual positivity/negativity.

items.title: These strange pink rocks just revealed a hidden giant beneath Antarctica
items.summary: Pink granite boulders sitting mysteriously atop Antarctica’s Hudson Mountains have led scientists to a stunning discovery: a hidden granite mass buried beneath Pine Island Glacier, stretching nearly 100 km wide and 7 km thick. By dating the rocks to the Jurassic period and matching them with gravity signals detected from aircraft, researchers solved a decades-old puzzle about their origin.

items.title: These dinosaurs had wings but couldn’t fly
items.summary: Some feathered dinosaurs may have briefly taken to the skies—only to give it up later. By studying rare fossils with preserved feathers, researchers uncovered a surprising clue hidden in molting patterns, revealing that Anchiornis likely couldn’t fly at all. Instead of the neat, symmetrical feather replacement seen in flying birds, these dinosaurs showed a messy, irregular molt—something only flightless animals exhibit.

items.title: JWST reveals a strange sulfur world unlike any planet we know
items.summary: Astronomers have identified a strange new kind of exoplanet that challenges how scientists classify worlds beyond our Solar System. The planet, L 98-59 d, appears to contain a vast ocean of molten rock beneath its surface that traps large amounts of sulfur deep inside. Observations from the James Webb Space Telescope revealed unusual sulfur-rich gases in its atmosphere and a surprisingly low density for its size.

items.title: MIT scientists finally see hidden quantum “jiggling” inside superconductors
items.summary: MIT physicists have built a powerful new microscope that uses terahertz light to uncover hidden quantum motions inside superconductors. By compressing this normally unwieldy light into a tiny region, they were able to observe electrons moving together in a frictionless, wave-like state for the first time. This discovery opens a new window into how superconductors really work. It could also help drive future breakthroughs in high-speed wireless communication.

items.title: He survived 48 hours without lungs and lived
items.summary: A critically ill 33-year-old man survived an almost unimaginable scenario—living for 48 hours without lungs—thanks to a groundbreaking surgical approach. After a severe flu-triggered infection destroyed his lungs and caused multiple organ failure, doctors removed both lungs entirely to stop the spread of infection. In their place, they used a specially engineered “artificial lung” system to oxygenate his blood and keep his body functioning while he stabilized.

items.title: Even JWST can’t see through this planet’s massive haze
items.summary: Kepler-51d is a giant, ultra-light “super-puff” planet wrapped in an unusually thick haze that’s blocking scientists from seeing what it’s made of. Observations from JWST revealed that this haze may be one of the largest ever detected, possibly stretching as wide as Earth itself. The planet’s low density and close orbit don’t match existing models of how gas giants form or survive. Now, researchers are left with more questions than answers about how such a strange world came to be.

items.title: This massive crater could expose the heart of a lost planet
items.summary: A mysterious metal-rich asteroid called Psyche has been baffling scientists for over two centuries, and its true origin remains one of the biggest unanswered questions in planetary science. Is it the exposed core of a failed planet, or a chaotic mix of rock and metal forged through countless violent collisions? To find out, researchers simulated how a massive crater near Psyche’s north pole formed, revealing that the asteroid’s internal “porosity” — how much empty space it contains — may hold th

items.title: Scientists just discovered bull sharks have friends
items.summary: Bull sharks may have a reputation as lone hunters, but new research reveals they actually form social bonds and even have preferred “friends.” After six years of observing 184 sharks in Fiji, scientists discovered these animals don’t just mix randomly—they choose companions, swim together, and even follow one another in coordinated ways.

items.title: NASA’s Webb captures a bizarre brain-shaped nebula around a dying star
items.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has revealed new details in a bizarre nebula that looks like a brain floating in space. Formed by a dying star, the “Exposed Cranium” nebula shows layered gas and a dark central divide that creates its eerie shape. Webb’s infrared view suggests powerful jets may be shaping the structure. The images capture a brief and dramatic phase in a star’s final evolution.

items.title: The smell of Egyptian mummies is revealing 2,000-year-old secrets
items.summary: The distinctive smell of ancient mummies is helping scientists decode the secrets of Egyptian mummification. By analyzing tiny traces of chemicals in the air around mummy samples, researchers identified dozens of compounds linked to oils, resins, beeswax, and bitumen used during embalming. The chemical clues reveal that mummification grew increasingly complex over thousands of years.

items.title: Rare supernova from 10 billion years ago may reveal the secret of dark energy
items.summary: Astronomers may have found an exciting new clue about dark energy—the mysterious force driving the universe’s accelerating expansion. They discovered an extraordinarily bright supernova from more than 10 billion years ago whose light was bent and magnified by a foreground galaxy, creating multiple images through gravitational lensing. Because the light from each image traveled slightly different paths, it arrived at Earth at different times, letting scientists effectively watch different moments

items.title: A strange twist in the universe’s oldest light may be bigger than we thought
items.summary: Scientists studying a mysterious effect called cosmic birefringence—a subtle twist in the polarization of the universe’s oldest light—have developed a new way to reduce uncertainty in how it’s measured. This faint rotation in the cosmic microwave background could point to entirely new physics, including hidden particles such as axions and clues about dark matter or dark energy.
